strengths: Actively building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling at re:cinq, directly satisfying the AI agent development requirement
Deep TypeScript/Node.js/React/AWS/MongoDB stack demonstrated across multiple senior roles (re:cinq, Optilyz) with production scale (millions of mail sends, millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Strong testing culture evidenced by open-source test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial test runner), TDD practice at RIDE Capital, and organized testing workshops and dojos
concerns: Nest.js — the listed primary backend framework — appears nowhere in the resume; ramp-up time is unknown for a role where it is a core requirement
Kotlin/Java exposure is minimal and dated: Java last used for robotic arms at Altom (2016–2018); the secondary stack requirement is barely satisfied
PostgreSQL is absent from the resume; SQL is listed as a skill but without concrete project evidence; database depth outside MongoDB is unverified

key_factors.factor: TypeScript / Node.js / React / AWS stack alignment

response.justification: Bogdan satisfies the two competencies that most differentiate this role: he is actively building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, and he has demonstrated TypeScript/Node.js/React seniority with AWS and MongoDB at production scale. His 15+ years of breadth across microservices, embedded, mobile, and GIS systems supports the architecture and scalability responsibilities. The primary gap is Nest.js — it is the listed primary backend framework and never appears in the resume — which is a real, if likely bridgeable, ramp-up risk given his strong Node.js foundation. Kotlin/Java is weak, and relational database depth (PostgreSQL) is unverified. Evidence-based score reflects a high-fit candidate with one meaningful framework gap.
